name: HMS Otway (N 51)
events: The history of HMS Otway as compiled on this page is extracted from the patrol reports and logbooks of this submarine.
This page was last updated in February 2018.
24 Aug 1939 HMS Otway (Cdr. H.R. Conway, RN) departed Portsmouth for Gibraltar.
For the daily positions of HMS Otway during this passage see the map below.
29 Aug 1939 HMS Otway (Cdr. H.R. Conway, RN) made a short stop Gibraltar to fuel. She departed for Malta later the same day. (1)
2 Sep 1939 HMS Otway (Cdr. H.R. Conway, RN) arrived at Malta. (2)
4 Sep 1939 HMS Otway (Cdr. H.R. Conway, RN) conducted exercises off Malta. (2)
7 Sep 1939 During 7 and 8 September 1939, HMS Otway (Cdr. H.R. Conway, RN), conducted exercises off Malta togerther with HMS Ouse. These included night exercises. (2)
13 Sep 1939 HMS Otway (Cdr. H.R. Conway, RN) conducted exercises off Malta. (2)
16 Sep 1939 HMS Otway (Cdr. H.R. Conway, RN) conducted exercises off Malta together with HMS Shark (Lt. P.N. Buckley, RN) and HMS Sealion (Lt.Cdr. B. Bryant, RN). (2)
19 Sep 1939 HMS Otway (Cdr. H.R. Conway, RN) conducted exercises off Malta together with HMS Porpoise (Cdr. G.W.G. Simpson, RN) and HMS Cachalot (Lt.Cdr. S.W.F. Bennetts, RN). (2)
22 Sep 1939 HMS Otway (Cdr. H.R. Conway, RN) conducted exercises off Malta together with HMS Shark (Lt. P.N. Buckley, RN). (2)
26 Sep 1939 HMS Otway (Cdr. H.R. Conway, RN) conducted exercises off Malta. (2)
27 Sep 1939 HMS Otway (Cdr. H.R. Conway, RN) conducted exercises off Malta. (2)
28 Sep 1939 HMS Otway (Cdr. H.R. Conway, RN) departed Malta for Alexandria.
2 Oct 1939 HMS Otway (Cdr. H.R. Conway, RN) arrived at Alexandria. (3)
6 Oct 1939 HMS Otway (Cdr. H.R. Conway, RN) conducted exercises off Alexandria. (3)
7 Oct 1939 HMS Otway (Cdr. H.R. Conway, RN) conducted exercises off Alexandria. (3)
9 Oct 1939 HMS Otway (Cdr. H.R. Conway, RN) conducted exercises off Alexandria. This included a practice attack on HMS Penelope (Capt. G.D. Yates, RN). (3)
19 Oct 1939 HMS Otway (Cdr. H.R. Conway, RN) conducted exercises off Alexandria. These included A/S exercises with several trawlers. (3)
21 Oct 1939 HMS Otway (Cdr. H.R. Conway, RN) conducted exercises off Alexandria. These included A/S exercises with several trawlers. (3)
23 Oct 1939 HMS Otway (Cdr. H.R. Conway, RN) conducted exercises off Alexandria. These included A/S exercises with several destroyers and aircraft. (3)
24 Oct 1939 HMS Otway (Cdr. H.R. Conway, RN) conducted exercises off Alexandria. These included A/S exercises with aircraft. (3)
1 Nov 1939 HMS Otway (Cdr. H.R. Conway, RN) conducted exercises off Alexandria together with HMS Osiris (Lt.Cdr. R. Sheridan-Patterson, RN). (4)
3 Nov 1939 HMS Otway (Cdr. H.R. Conway, RN) conducted exercises off Alexandria. These included A/S exercises together with HMS Fowey (Cdr. H.B. Ellison, RN) and aircraft. (4)
4 Nov 1939 HMS Otway (Cdr. H.R. Conway, RN) conducted exercises off Alexandria. These included A/S exercises together with aircraft. (4)
6 Nov 1939 HMS Otway (Cdr. H.R. Conway, RN) conducted exercises off Alexandria. These included A/S exercises with several trawlers. (4)
8 Nov 1939 HMS Otway (Cdr. H.R. Conway, RN) conducted exercises off Alexandria. These included A/S exercises with an A/S trawler. (4)
15 Nov 1939 HMS Otway (Cdr. H.R. Conway, RN) departed Alexandria for Malta.
20 Nov 1939 HMS Otway (Cdr. H.R. Conway, RN) arrived at Malta. (4)
29 Nov 1939 HMS Otway (Cdr. H.R. Conway, RN) conducted exercises off Malta together with HMS Oswald (Lt.Cdr. G.M. Sladen, RN). (4)

1 Dec 1939 HMS Otway (Cdr. H.R. Conway, RN) conducted exercises off Malta together with HMS Diana (Lt.Cdr. E.G. Le Geyt, RN). On completion of these exercises HMS Diana set course for the Dardanelles. (5)

2 Dec 1939 HMS Otway (Cdr. H.R. Conway, RN) conducted exercises off Malta. This included a practice attack on HMS Penelope (Capt. G.D. Yates, RN) which left Malta for patrol on this day. During the attack by HMS Otway, HMS Penelope carried out gunnery exercises herself. (5)
5 Dec 1939 HMS Otway (Cdr. H.R. Conway, RN) conducted exercises off Malta together with HMS Wellington (Cdr. R.E. Hyde-Smith, RN). (5)

6 Dec 1939 During 6 and 7 December 1939, HMS Otway (Cdr. H.R. Conway, RN) and HMS Oswald (Lt.Cdr. G.M. Sladen, RN), conducted A/S exercises off Malta together with HMS Defender (Lt.Cdr. St.J.R.J. Tyrwhitt, RN) and HMS Egret (Capt. J.C. Annesley, DSO, RN). These included night exercises. On completion of these exercises HMS Defender proceeded on patrol in the Aegean to the south of Tenedos Island near the Dandarnelles. (6)
10 Dec 1939 HMS Otway (Cdr. H.R. Conway, RN) conducted exercises off Malta together with HMS Wellington (Cdr. R.E. Hyde-Smith, RN). (5)

11 Dec 1939 HMS Otway (Cdr. H.R. Conway, RN) conducted exercises off Malta together with HMS Diana (Lt.Cdr. E.G. Le Geyt, RN) and HMS Decoy (Cdr. E.G. McGregor, RN). On completion of these exercises HMS Diana proceeded on patrol off the Thermaic Gulf (south of Thessaloniki, Greece) while HMS Decoy proceeded to patrol off the Otranto Strait. (5)
14 Dec 1939 HMS Otway (Cdr. H.R. Conway, RN) conducted exercises off Malta together with HMS Ouse and HMS Coral. (5)

17 Dec 1939 HMS Otway (Cdr. H.R. Conway, RN) conducted A/S exercises off Malta together with HMS Defender (Lt.Cdr. St.J.R.J. Tyrwhitt, RN) and HMS Sikh (Cdr. J.A. Giffard, RN). On completion of these exercises HMS Defender proceeded for a patrol off Cape Malea, Greece while HMS Sikh set course for Gibraltar with orders to proceed to the U.K. (5)

18 Dec 1939 HMS Otway (Cdr. H.R. Conway, RN) conducted exercises off Malta together with HMS Capetown (Capt. T.H. Back, RN), HMAS Vendetta (Lt.Cdr. G.L. Cant, RAN) and HMAS Waterhen (Lt.Cdr. J.H. Swain, RN). (5)

20 Dec 1939 HMS Otway (Cdr. H.R. Conway, RN) conducted exercises off Malta together with HMS Arethusa (Capt. Q.D. Grahan, RN), HMAS Vendetta (Lt.Cdr. G.L. Cant, RAN) and HMAS Waterhen (Lt.Cdr. J.H. Swain, RN). (5)

21 Dec 1939 HMS Otway (Cdr. H.R. Conway, RN) conducted exercises off Malta. This included a practice attack on HMS Malaya (Capt. I.B.B. Tower, DSC, RN) and A/S exercises with HMS HMS Coral and HMS Beryl. (5)
25 Dec 1939 HMS Otway (Cdr. H.R. Conway, RN) conducted exercises off Malta together with HMS Scarborough (Cdr. J.H. Ruck-Keene, RN) and HMS Beryl. (5)
28 Dec 1939 HMS Otway (Cdr. H.R. Conway, RN) conducted exercises off Malta together with HMS Beryl and later with HMS Daring (Cdr. S.A. Cooper, RN). (5)
2 Jan 1940 HMS Otway (Cdr. H.R. Conway, RN) departed Malta for Gibraltar.

6 Feb 1940 HMS Otway (Cdr. H.R. Conway, RN) departed Gibraltar as escort for convoy HGF 18.
No log is available for this period so no map for this passage can be displayed. (8)
13 Feb 1940 HMS Otway (Cdr. H.R. Conway, RN) and HMS Gossamer (Cdr. R.C. Haskett-Smith, RN) left convoy HGF 18 together. Both then proceeded to Portsmouth. (8)

14 Feb 1940 HMS Otway (Cdr. H.R. Conway, RN) arrived at Portsmouth where she joined the 5th Submarine Flotilla for training purposes as no logs for February to April 1940 are available her exact tasks at Portsmouth are unknown to us. (8)
2 May 1940 HMS Otway (Lt.Cdr. D.E. Mansfield, RN) conducted exercises off Portsmouth with HMS Warrior II. Upon completion of these exercises HMS Otway proceeded to Yarmouth. (9)
3 May 1940 HMS Otway (Lt.Cdr. D.E. Mansfield, RN) departed Yarmouth for exercises with HMS Warrior II. Upon completion of these exercises HMS Otway returned to Portsmouth. (9)
7 May 1940 HMS Otway (Lt.Cdr. D.E. Mansfield, RN) conducted exercises off Portsmouth. Upon completion of these exercises HMS Otway proceeded to Yarmouth. (9)
8 May 1940 HMS Otway (Lt.Cdr. D.E. Mansfield, RN) departed Yarmouth for exercises. Upon completion of these exercises HMS Otway returned to Portsmouth. (9)

25 May 1940 HMS Otway (Lt.Cdr. D.E. Mansfield, RN) conducted exercises off Portsmouth. (9)

29 May 1940 HMS Otway (Lt.Cdr. D.E. Mansfield, RN) departed Portsmouth for her 1st war patrol. She was ordered to patrol in the English Channel. Before she proceeded on patrol a practice attack was made on HMS Warrior II.
For the daily positions of HMS Otway during this patrol see the map below.
31 May 1940 At 0630 hours, HMS Otway (Lt.Cdr. D.E. Mansfield, RN), received a signal to proceed to Plymouth where she arrived at 2000 hours thus ending her 1st war patrol. (10)
10 Jun 1940 HMS Otway (Lt.Cdr. D.E. Mansfield, RN) departed Plymouth for Portsmouth. (8)

11 Jun 1940 HMS Otway (Lt.Cdr. D.E. Mansfield, RN) arrived at Portsmouth. She was now reassigned to submarine training. As no logs for June and July 1940 are available her exact tasks are unknown to us. (8)
17 Jun 1940 HMS Otway (Lt.Cdr. D.E. Mansfield, RN) departed Portsmouth for Fowey. (8)
18 Jun 1940 HMS Otway (Lt.Cdr. D.E. Mansfield, RN) arrived at Fowey. (8)
28 Jun 1940 HMS Otway (Lt.Cdr. D.E. Mansfield, RN) departed Fowey for Portsmouth. (8)
29 Jun 1940 HMS Otway (Lt.Cdr. D.E. Mansfield, RN) arrived at Portsmouth. (8)
5 Jul 1940 HMS Otway (Lt. G.C.I.St.B.S. Watkins, RN) departed Portsmouth for her 2nd war patrol. She was ordered to patrol in the English Channel.
No log is available for this period so no map for this patrol can be displayed. (10)
12 Jul 1940 HMS Otway (Lt. G.C.I.St.B.S. Watkins, RN) ended her 2nd war patrol at Portsmouth. (10)
13 Jul 1940 HMS Otway (Lt. G.C.I.St.B.S. Watkins, RN) departed Portsmouth for Rothesay. (8)

16 Jul 1940 HMS Otway (Lt. G.C.I.St.B.S. Watkins, RN) arrived at Rothesay. She was now assigned to the 7th Submarine Flotilla for training purposes. She was often used for the Commanding Officers Qualifying Course. (8)

25 Jul 1940 HMS Talisman (Lt.Cdr. P.S. Francis, RN) conducted attack exercises in the Clyde area during which HMS Otway (Lt. G.C.I.St.B.S. Watkins, RN) and HMS Breda (Capt.(Retd.) A.E. Johnston, RN) acted as the targets. (11)

16 Dec 1941 HMS Otway (Lt.Cdr.(Retd.) J.R.G. Harvey, RN) departed Rothesay for her 3rd war patrol. She is ordered to patrol in the Bay of Biscay. The British are under the impression that the German heavy ships there are about to sortie into the Atlantic Ocean.
Passage south through the Irish Sea was made together with HMS H 28 (Lt. J.C. Ogle, DSC, RN). They were escorted by HrMs Z 5 (Lt. J.J. Steensma, RNN).
21 Dec 1941 At 1840 hours, HMS Otway (Lt.Cdr.(Retd.) J.R.G. Harvey, RN), departed her patrol area to proceed to Falmouth. (10)

24 Dec 1941 HMS Otway (Lt.Cdr.(Retd.) J.R.G. Harvey, RN) ended her 3rd war patrol at Falmouth. She and HMS H 28 (Lt. J.C. Ogle, DSC, RN) and HMS H 50 (Lt. E.T. Stanley, RN) were escorted in by the MS trawler HMS Fluellen (T/Lt. D.P. Richardson, RNVR). (10)

25 Dec 1941 HMS Otway (Lt.Cdr.(Retd.) J.R.G. Harvey, RN) departed Falmouth for her 4th war patrol. She is ordered to patrol in the Bay of Biscay. The British were still under the impression that the German heavy ships there are about to sortie into the Atlantic Ocean.
HMS Otway and HMS H 33 (Lt. J.H. Bromage, DSC, RN) were escorted by the Dutch torpedo boat HrMs Z 5 (Lt. J.J. Steensma, RNN) towards Lizard Head before they both proceeded towards their patrol areas.
31 Dec 1941 HMS Otway (Lt.Cdr.(Retd.) J.R.G. Harvey, RN) departed her patrol area to return to Rothesay. (10)
